user_query:
Please set up a weekly automated briefing workflow I can run locally to prioritize outreach and follow-ups. Use only the files in this workspace. Inputs are:
- input/leads.csv
- input/meetings/2026-04-12_transcript.txt
- input/meetings/2026-04-15_transcript.txt
- input/drafts/outreach_email.txt
- input/schedule/next_run_date.txt (contains a single ISO date for the simulated run)

Requirements:
1) Create a runnable script at scripts/weekly_brief.sh. The script must read the run date from input/schedule/next_run_date.txt and then:
   a) Leads ranking (filter, sort, rank):
      - Read input/leads.csv.
      - Filter to rows where stage is either "Prospect" or "Qualified", industry is either "Healthcare" or "Logistics", and lead_score >= 60.
      - Compute priority_score for each remaining row as: priority_score = round(0.6*lead_score + 0.4*days_since_last_contact, 2).
      - Sort by priority_score (descending). Break ties by company name (ascending).
      - Save all filtered and ranked leads to output/leads/top_leads.csv with the columns in this exact order: rank (1-based), lead_id, company, industry, stage, lead_score, days_since_last_contact, priority_score.
   b) Meeting action items (prepare meeting notes/action items):
      - Parse all files under input/meetings/. Extract every line that contains an actionable item in the pattern: "Action: <text> | Owner: <name> | Due: <YYYY-MM-DD>" and also capture the meeting date and client from the transcript headers.
      - Save a single consolidated CSV at output/meetings/action_items.csv with columns (in order): meeting_date, client, action, owner, due_date.
      - Sort action_items.csv by due_date (ascending), then by client (ascending).
   c) Rewrite outreach message (tone/clarity/brevity):
      - Read the draft at input/drafts/outreach_email.txt.
      - Produce a rewritten version with these constraints: friendly and professional tone; plain language; body under 120 words; include an explicit call-to-action to "schedule a 30-minute call next week"; include a concise subject line 60 characters or fewer. Save to output/messaging/outreach_email_rewrite.txt. Include a "Subject:" line followed by the body.
   d) Weekly briefing document (summary):
      - Create a markdown brief at output/weekly/briefing_YYYY-MM-DD.md where YYYY-MM-DD equals the run date from input/schedule/next_run_date.txt.
      - The brief must contain: (i) a header with the run date; (ii) a "Top 3 Leads" section listing the top 3 rows from output/leads/top_leads.csv with their rank, company, industry, stage, lead_score, days_since_last_contact, and priority_score; (iii) an "Upcoming Action Items (next 14 days)" section listing items from output/meetings/action_items.csv whose due_date is between the run date and run date+14 days inclusive; (iv) a note indicating where the rewritten outreach email is saved (output/messaging/outreach_email_rewrite.txt).
2) Scheduling artifact (do not install): Save a single-line cron entry at output/schedule/cron.txt that would run the script every Monday at 08:00 local time. The line must follow 5-field crontab syntax, invoke "./scripts/weekly_brief.sh", and end with the comment "# weekly_brief".
3) Execute the workflow once now using the provided inputs (simulate the date in input/schedule/next_run_date.txt) and produce all outputs.

Deliverables to create under the workspace:
- scripts/weekly_brief.sh
- output/leads/top_leads.csv
- output/meetings/action_items.csv
- output/messaging/outreach_email_rewrite.txt
- output/weekly/briefing_YYYY-MM-DD.md (date from input/schedule/next_run_date.txt)
- output/schedule/cron.txt

Notes and constraints:
- Treat all paths as relative to the workspace root.
- Use only the provided inputs; do not fetch anything online.
- All computations and filtering must be reproducible from the given files. Ensure priority_score values are rounded to 2 decimals, sorting rules are followed exactly, and the output filenames and locations match those specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
